title: Day 14 Vocabulary — Use of Let's (100 Words)

words.simpleMeaning: to propose an idea or plan

sentences.daily: I suggest we go for a walk.
sentences.office: Let's suggest a new approach to the team.
sentences.interview: I always suggest improvements politely.
sentences.business: We suggest meeting the client next week.
sentences.formal: Let us suggest a more efficient method.
sentences.informal: Why don't we suggest a movie?

words.usageNote: 'Suggest' is often used with 'Let's' in making proposals: 'I suggest we.../ Let's...'

words.simpleMeaning: to decide in advance what to do

sentences.interview: I always plan my answers before speaking.
sentences.formal: Let us plan the annual event carefully.
sentences.informal: Let's plan something fun for tonight!

words.usageNote: 'Plan' as a noun: 'We have a plan.' As a verb: 'Let's plan together.'

words.simpleMeaning: to come together with someone

sentences.daily: Let's meet at the coffee shop.
sentences.office: Let's meet at 10 AM to discuss this.
sentences.interview: I look forward to meeting you.
sentences.business: Let's meet the client on Friday.
sentences.formal: Let us meet and resolve this matter.
sentences.informal: Let's meet up this weekend!

words.usageNote: Irregular: meet → met → met. 'Meet up' is informal for casual meetings.

words.simpleMeaning: to talk about something in detail

sentences.interview: I am open to discussing my experience.
sentences.formal: Let us discuss this matter in detail.
sentences.informal: Let's discuss it later, okay?

words.usageNote: 'Discuss about' is WRONG. Say 'discuss this' (not 'discuss about this').

words.simpleMeaning: to make a choice after thinking

sentences.daily: Let's decide where to eat tonight.
sentences.office: Let's decide on the final design.
sentences.interview: I can decide quickly under pressure.
sentences.formal: Let us decide on a course of action.
sentences.informal: Let's just decide already!

words.usageNote: 'Decide on' = make a specific choice. 'Decide to' = resolve to do something.

words.simpleMeaning: to do something enjoyable for a special occasion

sentences.interview: I like to celebrate small wins.
sentences.formal: Let us celebrate this achievement.
sentences.informal: Let's celebrate tonight!

words.usageNote: Used for positive occasions. 'Celebrate' is always followed by the event or person.

words.simpleMeaning: to examine or travel to discover new things

sentences.daily: Let's explore the new park nearby.
sentences.interview: I love to explore new ideas.
sentences.formal: Let us explore the available options.
sentences.informal: Let's explore the old city this Sunday!

words.usageNote: 'Explore' is used for both physical travel and abstract ideas.

words.simpleMeaning: to work jointly with others

sentences.daily: Let's collaborate on this school project.
sentences.interview: I enjoy collaborating with diverse teams.
sentences.formal: Let us collaborate to achieve our goals.
sentences.informal: Let's collaborate — it'll be more fun!

words.usageNote: More formal than 'work together'. Common in professional/business contexts.

words.simpleMeaning: to find a solution to a problem

sentences.office: Let's resolve this issue before the client arrives.
sentences.interview: I know how to resolve conflicts professionally.
sentences.formal: Let us resolve this matter amicably.
sentences.informal: Let's just resolve it and move on.

words.usageNote: 'Resolve a conflict/issue/dispute' are very common collocations.

words.simpleMeaning: to arrange things in an orderly way

sentences.interview: I organize my work with a priority list.
sentences.business: Let's organize a client appreciation event.
sentences.formal: Let us organize the proceedings systematically.
sentences.informal: Let's organize a surprise party!

words.usageNote: British spelling: 'organise'. Both are correct. 'Organize' is most common in professional settings.

words.simpleMeaning: to make something better

sentences.office: Let's improve our customer response time.
sentences.interview: I am always looking to improve my skills.
sentences.formal: Let us improve our processes.
sentences.informal: Let's improve together!

words.usageNote: 'Improve on' = do better than a previous result. 'Improve' alone = make better.

words.simpleMeaning: to concentrate on something

sentences.interview: I can focus well under pressure.
sentences.formal: Let us focus our efforts on this objective.
sentences.informal: Let's focus, guys!

words.usageNote: 'Focus on' is the correct preposition. 'Focus at' is incorrect.

words.simpleMeaning: to make ready in advance

sentences.office: Let's prepare a presentation for Monday.
sentences.interview: I always prepare thoroughly for interviews.
sentences.business: Let's prepare a proposal for the client.
sentences.formal: Let us prepare a comprehensive report.
sentences.informal: Let's prepare before it's too late!

words.usageNote: 'Prepare for' = get ready for an event. 'Prepare' someone = to train/ready them.

words.simpleMeaning: to examine or assess something again

sentences.daily: Let's review what we learned today.
sentences.office: Let's review the contract before signing.
sentences.interview: I review my performance regularly.
sentences.formal: Let us review the terms and conditions.
sentences.informal: Let's review quickly before the test!

words.usageNote: 'Review' as noun: 'a performance review'. As verb: 'Let's review the plan.'

words.simpleMeaning: to give part of something to others

sentences.office: Let's share our updates in the standup.
sentences.interview: I love sharing knowledge with my team.
sentences.formal: Let us share the responsibilities fairly.
sentences.informal: Let's share this pizza!

words.usageNote: 'Share with' = give to someone. 'Share' in digital context = to post/forward content.

words.simpleMeaning: to take pleasure in something

sentences.interview: I enjoy challenging assignments.
sentences.formal: Let us enjoy the fruits of our hard work.
sentences.informal: Let's enjoy ourselves tonight!

words.usageNote: 'Enjoy' is always followed by a noun or gerund (ing), not 'to': Enjoy doing (✓), Enjoy to do (✗).

words.simpleMeaning: to rest and stop worrying

sentences.office: Let's relax after the project submission.
sentences.interview: I know how to relax between tasks.
sentences.business: Let's relax the rules a little for this event.
sentences.formal: Let us relax the timeline slightly.
sentences.informal: Let's relax — it's the weekend!

words.usageNote: 'Relax' is often used informally as advice: 'Relax! Everything will be fine.'

words.simpleMeaning: to accept something as true without proof

sentences.office: Let's not assume what the client wants — let's ask.
sentences.interview: I never assume; I ask for clarification.
sentences.business: Let's not assume the deal is closed.
sentences.formal: Let us not assume without evidence.
sentences.informal: Let's not assume things!

words.usageNote: 'Assume' = take for granted. 'Assume responsibility' = accept responsibility formally.

words.simpleMeaning: to make something definite; to verify

sentences.daily: Let's confirm the time before going.
sentences.interview: I always confirm important details in writing.
sentences.business: Let's confirm the order with the supplier.
sentences.formal: Let us confirm the arrangements.
sentences.informal: Let's confirm the plan tonight.

words.usageNote: 'Confirm' in business emails: 'I am writing to confirm...' is very standard.

words.simpleMeaning: to continue or move forward with an action

sentences.office: Let's proceed to the next agenda item.
sentences.interview: I know when to proceed and when to pause.
sentences.formal: Let us proceed in an orderly manner.
sentences.informal: Let's proceed — no time to waste!

words.usageNote: Very formal word. In meetings: 'Let's proceed' is professional and polished.

words.simpleMeaning: to complete a task

sentences.office: Let's finish the report before 5 PM.
sentences.interview: I always finish tasks before deadlines.
sentences.formal: Let us finish what we started.
sentences.informal: Let's finish eating first!

words.usageNote: 'Finish + ing': Let's finish eating. Always gerund after 'finish', not infinitive.

words.simpleMeaning: to agree to receive or do something

sentences.daily: Let's accept our mistakes and move on.
sentences.interview: I accept challenges with a positive mindset.
sentences.formal: Let us accept the terms of the agreement.
sentences.informal: Let's just accept it and move on.

words.usageNote: 'Accept' vs 'except': Accept = to receive/agree. Except = excluding. Common confusion!

words.simpleMeaning: to stay away from someone or something

sentences.daily: Let's avoid junk food this week.
sentences.interview: I avoid giving vague answers.
sentences.formal: Let us avoid any further complications.
sentences.informal: Let's avoid that topic — it gets heated!

words.usageNote: 'Avoid + ing': Let's avoid wasting time. Always gerund after 'avoid'.

words.simpleMeaning: to make something known publicly

sentences.daily: Let's announce the good news together.
sentences.office: Let's announce the new policy tomorrow.
sentences.interview: I was asked to announce the results.
sentences.business: Let's announce the product launch date.
sentences.formal: Let us announce the winners.
sentences.informal: Let's announce our engagement!

words.usageNote: Used for formal public statements. 'Announce to' = tell a group. 'Announce that' = introduce a fact.

words.simpleMeaning: to recognize the value of something

sentences.office: Let's appreciate our team's hard work.
sentences.interview: I appreciate constructive feedback.
sentences.formal: Let us appreciate the efforts of all involved.
sentences.informal: Let's appreciate each other more!

words.usageNote: 'I appreciate it' is a polite way to say thank you. Very useful in professional English.

words.simpleMeaning: to pledge to do something; to carry out an action

sentences.daily: Let's commit to waking up early.
sentences.interview: I commit fully to any task assigned.
sentences.formal: Let us commit to excellence.
sentences.informal: Let's commit and not back out this time!

words.usageNote: 'Commit to + noun/gerund': commit to improvement / commit to improving.

words.simpleMeaning: to question or test something; a difficult task

sentences.daily: Let's challenge ourselves to do better.
sentences.office: Let's challenge the old way of doing things.
sentences.interview: I welcome being challenged.
sentences.formal: Let us challenge the assumptions.
sentences.informal: Let's challenge each other — who's faster?

words.usageNote: 'Challenge' as noun: 'It's a big challenge.' As verb: 'Let's challenge our limits.'

words.simpleMeaning: to come together or collect things

sentences.office: Let's gather all the data first.
sentences.interview: I can gather information quickly.
sentences.formal: Let us gather the necessary documents.
sentences.informal: Let's gather everyone and start the party!

words.usageNote: 'Gather' is used for both people (assemble) and things (collect).

words.simpleMeaning: to start something new; to introduce a product

sentences.daily: Let's launch the new app today.
sentences.interview: I have experience in launching new products.
sentences.business: Let's launch in the metro cities first.
sentences.formal: Let us launch this initiative officially.
sentences.informal: Let's launch it — we've been waiting long enough!

words.usageNote: 'Product launch', 'app launch', 'campaign launch' — very common in business English.

words.simpleMeaning: to assign a task to someone else

sentences.office: Let's delegate this task to Priya.
sentences.interview: I know when to delegate and when to do it myself.
sentences.formal: Let us delegate tasks according to expertise.
sentences.informal: Let's delegate some work — we're overloaded!

words.usageNote: Good managers delegate. 'Delegate to' = assign task to someone: 'delegate to the team'.

words.simpleMeaning: to give someone a reason to do something

sentences.interview: I can motivate a team in tough times.
sentences.business: Let's motivate customers with better offers.
sentences.formal: Let us motivate our colleagues.
sentences.informal: Let's motivate ourselves first!

words.usageNote: 'Motivated' (adjective): a motivated employee. 'Motivating' (adjective): a motivating speech.

words.simpleMeaning: to reach an agreement through discussion

sentences.office: Let's negotiate the terms with the vendor.
sentences.interview: I am skilled at negotiating.
sentences.formal: Let us negotiate in good faith.
sentences.informal: Let's negotiate — nobody needs to lose!

words.usageNote: 'Negotiate a deal/contract/price/salary' are common collocations.

words.simpleMeaning: to ask someone to come or participate

sentences.daily: Let's invite our neighbors for dinner.
sentences.office: Let's invite the new team to the workshop.
sentences.interview: I was invited to present my ideas.
sentences.business: Let's invite the client for a demo.
sentences.formal: Let us invite all stakeholders.
sentences.informal: Let's invite everyone and make it a party!

words.usageNote: 'Invite to' = invite to an event/place. 'Invite for' = invite for a meal/occasion.

words.simpleMeaning: to offer to do something without being asked

sentences.daily: Let's volunteer at the local charity.
sentences.office: Let's volunteer for the new project.
sentences.interview: I always volunteer for challenging assignments.
sentences.business: Let's volunteer our team for this initiative.
sentences.formal: Let us volunteer our services.
sentences.informal: Let's volunteer — it feels good!

words.usageNote: 'Volunteer for' = offer to do a specific task. Noun: a volunteer at an NGO.

words.simpleMeaning: to make something easier to understand or do

sentences.interview: I always simplify complex ideas.
sentences.formal: Let us simplify the procedure.
sentences.informal: Let's simplify things — it's getting too complicated!

words.usageNote: 'Simplify' is very useful in office communication. Opposite: 'complicate'.

words.simpleMeaning: to think again about a previous decision

sentences.office: Let's reconsider the strategy before presenting.
sentences.interview: I always reconsider before giving a final answer.
sentences.business: Let's reconsider the terms of the deal.
sentences.formal: Let us reconsider our approach.
sentences.informal: Let's reconsider — I'm not sure it's the best idea.

words.usageNote: 'Reconsider' suggests changing your mind. Often used diplomatically in business.

words.simpleMeaning: to work jointly with others

sentences.daily: Let's collaborate on this art project.
sentences.interview: I thrive when I collaborate.
sentences.formal: Let us collaborate for mutual benefit.
sentences.informal: Let's collaborate — two heads are better than one!

words.usageNote: 'Collaborate with someone on something.' More formal than 'work together'.

words.simpleMeaning: to finish or conclude something

sentences.office: Let's wrap up the meeting in 5 minutes.
sentences.interview: Let me wrap up my answer.
sentences.formal: Let us wrap up the proceedings.
sentences.informal: Let's wrap up and go eat!

words.usageNote: 'Wrap up' is a phrasal verb. Very common in meetings and presentations.

words.simpleMeaning: to move or do something very quickly

sentences.daily: Let's not rush — we have time.
sentences.interview: I don't rush my answers; I think first.
sentences.business: Let's not rush the client into signing.
sentences.formal: Let us not rush this process.
sentences.informal: Let's not rush — quality matters!

words.usageNote: 'Rush into' = do hastily: 'Don't rush into a decision.' 'Rush hour' = peak traffic time.

words.simpleMeaning: to put tasks in order of importance

sentences.daily: Let's prioritize our tasks for the day.
sentences.office: Let's prioritize the urgent client requests.
sentences.interview: I know how to prioritize effectively.
sentences.formal: Let us prioritize based on impact.
sentences.informal: Let's prioritize — we can't do everything at once!

words.usageNote: 'Prioritize' is essential in professional English. 'High-priority task' is a common phrase.

words.simpleMeaning: an idea or proposal put forward for consideration

sentences.interview: I always welcome suggestions.
sentences.formal: Please submit your suggestions.
sentences.informal: Any suggestions for the weekend?

words.usageNote: 'Make a suggestion' is the standard collocation. 'Give a suggestion' is also used.

words.simpleMeaning: a formal plan or suggestion

sentences.office: Let's submit the proposal by Friday.
sentences.interview: I prepared a proposal for the new role.
sentences.business: Let's send the proposal to the client.
sentences.formal: Let us review the proposal carefully.
sentences.informal: So what's your proposal?

words.usageNote: 'Business proposal', 'project proposal', 'marriage proposal' — context changes meaning.

words.simpleMeaning: a shared decision or contract between parties

sentences.interview: I always ensure mutual agreement.
sentences.formal: Let us draw up a formal agreement.
sentences.informal: Let's find an agreement we're both happy with.

words.usageNote: 'Come to an agreement', 'reach an agreement', 'sign an agreement' are all common.

words.simpleMeaning: working together toward a common goal

sentences.interview: Cooperation is key in my work style.
sentences.formal: Let us proceed with full cooperation.
sentences.informal: Let's have some cooperation here, please!

words.usageNote: 'Your cooperation is appreciated' is a very polite, formal phrase.

words.simpleMeaning: doing something well without wasting time or resources

sentences.daily: Let's find a more efficient way to do this.
sentences.office: Let's be more efficient with our time.
sentences.interview: I am an efficient worker.
sentences.formal: Let us adopt a more efficient process.
sentences.informal: Let's be efficient — we're running out of time!

words.usageNote: 'Efficient' = good use of resources. 'Effective' = achieves the goal. Both are important.

words.simpleMeaning: achieving a lot in a short time

sentences.interview: I am very productive when I have clear goals.
sentences.formal: Let us make this a productive session.
sentences.informal: Let's be productive today — no distractions!

words.usageNote: 'Productive' often collocates with 'day', 'meeting', 'session', 'conversation'.

words.simpleMeaning: truthful and sincere

sentences.daily: Let's be honest with each other.
sentences.office: Let's be honest about the challenges.
sentences.interview: I am always honest in my work.
sentences.business: Let's be honest about the risks.
sentences.formal: Let us be transparent and honest.
sentences.informal: Let's be honest — that didn't go well!

words.usageNote: 'Honest' is used in 'be honest', 'stay honest', 'honest feedback' — common in professional English.

words.simpleMeaning: concerned with real situations rather than theories

sentences.interview: I offer practical solutions.
sentences.formal: Let us be practical in our planning.
sentences.informal: Let's be practical — we don't have that budget!

words.usageNote: 'Practical' vs 'practice': practical (adj), practice (noun/verb). Common confusion.

words.simpleMeaning: having or showing original ideas

sentences.daily: Let's be more creative with our plans.
sentences.office: Let's have a creative brainstorming session.
sentences.interview: I am a creative problem-solver.
sentences.formal: Let us apply creative thinking.
sentences.informal: Let's get creative with this project!

words.usageNote: 'Creative' collocates with 'thinking', 'solution', 'approach', 'team'.

words.simpleMeaning: open and honest; easy to understand

sentences.daily: Let's be transparent about our plans.
sentences.interview: I believe in transparent work culture.
sentences.formal: Let us be transparent in our dealings.
sentences.informal: Let's just be transparent with each other.

words.usageNote: 'Transparency' is the noun. 'Transparent communication' is a key phrase in business.

words.simpleMeaning: a way of dealing with something; to move near

sentences.interview: My approach is always methodical.
sentences.business: Let's approach the problem from a new angle.
sentences.formal: Let us approach this matter with care.
sentences.informal: Let's approach it step by step.

words.usageNote: 'Approach' as noun: 'a new approach'. As verb: 'approach the problem'.

words.simpleMeaning: to accept or admit the truth of something

sentences.interview: I acknowledge areas where I need to improve.
sentences.formal: Let us acknowledge the contribution of all.
sentences.informal: Let's acknowledge the elephant in the room!

words.usageNote: 'Acknowledge' in emails: 'I acknowledge receipt of your message.' Very professional.

words.simpleMeaning: to state the main points briefly

sentences.interview: I always summarize my key strengths.
sentences.business: Let's summarize our offer for the client.
sentences.formal: Let us summarize the proceedings.
sentences.informal: Let's summarize quickly and move on.

words.usageNote: 'Summarize' at end of meetings: 'Let's summarize action items.' Very useful.

words.simpleMeaning: to think freely and generate many ideas

sentences.interview: I love brainstorming new approaches.
sentences.formal: Let us brainstorm possible strategies.
sentences.informal: Let's brainstorm — all ideas welcome!

words.usageNote: 'Brainstorming session' is very common in workplaces. No idea is bad in brainstorming.

words.simpleMeaning: to share information with others

sentences.office: Let's communicate the changes to the team.
sentences.interview: I communicate clearly and professionally.
sentences.formal: Let us communicate this policy change.
sentences.informal: Let's communicate openly — no secrets!

words.usageNote: 'Communicate with' = speak to. 'Communicate clearly' is a top skill employers look for.

words.simpleMeaning: to receive an advantage from something

sentences.office: Let's benefit from each other's expertise.
sentences.interview: My skills will benefit the team.
sentences.business: Let's find a solution that benefits both parties.
sentences.formal: Let us benefit from this collaboration.
sentences.informal: Let's benefit from this — it's a great opportunity!

words.usageNote: 'Benefit from' = gain something from. 'Benefit' as noun: 'the benefits of exercise'.

words.simpleMeaning: to successfully reach a goal

sentences.interview: I have a track record of achieving results.
sentences.formal: Let us achieve our collective objectives.
sentences.informal: Let's achieve great things together!

words.usageNote: 'Achieve a goal/target/result' are common collocations.

words.simpleMeaning: to put a plan or decision into action

sentences.office: Let's implement the new policy this week.
sentences.interview: I have experience implementing strategies.
sentences.formal: Let us implement the approved plan.
sentences.informal: Let's implement it — we've been talking long enough!

words.usageNote: 'Implement a plan/policy/solution/strategy' are core business collocations.

words.simpleMeaning: to give or receive the latest information

sentences.office: Let's update each other on progress.
sentences.interview: I regularly update my skills.
sentences.business: Let's update the client on the timeline.
sentences.formal: Let us update the records accordingly.
sentences.informal: Let's update our status on the group chat!

words.usageNote: 'Update someone on something' = inform about the latest. Very common in office English.

words.simpleMeaning: to plan something for a specific time

sentences.daily: Let's schedule a meeting for next week.
sentences.office: Let's schedule the review for Thursday.
sentences.interview: I always schedule time for preparation.
sentences.business: Let's schedule a call with the client.
sentences.formal: Let us schedule the presentation for Friday.
sentences.informal: Let's schedule something — we keep putting it off!

words.usageNote: 'Schedule' is both verb and noun. 'On schedule' = on time. 'Behind schedule' = late.

words.simpleMeaning: the latest time by which something must be done

sentences.interview: I always meet my deadlines.
sentences.formal: Let us adhere to the deadline.
sentences.informal: The deadline is tomorrow — let's hurry!

words.usageNote: 'Meet a deadline' = finish on time. 'Miss a deadline' = be late. Both are essential phrases.

words.simpleMeaning: cooperative effort by a group

sentences.interview: Teamwork is my greatest strength.
sentences.business: Let's build a culture of teamwork.
sentences.formal: Let us foster strong teamwork.
sentences.informal: Let's have better teamwork — we're all in this together!

words.usageNote: 'Teamwork makes the dream work' is a popular office saying. Used as uncountable noun.

words.simpleMeaning: the ability to start or lead something independently

sentences.interview: I always take initiative at work.
sentences.formal: Let us lead this initiative.
sentences.informal: Let's take initiative — don't wait to be told!

words.usageNote: 'Take the initiative' = act without being asked. Very valued quality in workplaces.

words.simpleMeaning: information about performance used to improve

sentences.daily: Let's give each other honest feedback.
sentences.office: Let's gather feedback from the team.
sentences.interview: I welcome feedback and act on it.
sentences.business: Let's use customer feedback to improve.
sentences.formal: Let us collect formal feedback.
sentences.informal: Let's give feedback — it only helps!

words.usageNote: 'Constructive feedback' = helpful criticism. 'Give feedback', 'receive feedback' are collocations.

words.simpleMeaning: a favorable time or situation for doing something

sentences.interview: I see this role as a great opportunity.
sentences.business: Let's turn this into a business opportunity.
sentences.formal: Let us leverage this opportunity.
sentences.informal: Let's grab the opportunity — it won't come again!

words.usageNote: 'Seize / grab / take an opportunity' are common collocations.

words.simpleMeaning: a plan designed to achieve a long-term goal

sentences.interview: I develop strategies for complex problems.
sentences.business: Let's align on strategy before proceeding.
sentences.formal: Let us formulate a sound strategy.
sentences.informal: Let's plan a strategy — winging it won't work!

words.usageNote: 'Business strategy', 'marketing strategy', 'exit strategy' are common types.

words.simpleMeaning: a difficult task that tests your abilities

sentences.interview: I enjoy tackling new challenges.
sentences.business: Let's turn this challenge into an opportunity.
sentences.formal: Let us overcome this challenge collectively.
sentences.informal: Let's face the challenge head-on!

words.usageNote: 'Face a challenge', 'overcome a challenge', 'meet a challenge' — common collocations.

words.simpleMeaning: to reach agreement by both sides giving something

sentences.interview: I know when to compromise professionally.
sentences.business: Let's compromise to close the deal.
sentences.formal: Let us reach a fair compromise.
sentences.informal: Let's compromise — nobody wins if we fight!

words.usageNote: 'Compromise on' = agree on a middle ground. 'Compromise quality' = lower the standard (negative).

words.simpleMeaning: to fill someone with the urge to do something creative

sentences.office: Let's inspire the team with positive energy.
sentences.interview: I want to inspire others through my work.
sentences.business: Let's inspire our clients to take action.
sentences.formal: Let us inspire a new generation.
sentences.informal: Let's inspire each other — every day!

words.usageNote: 'Inspire' someone to do something: 'Let's inspire them to work harder.'

words.simpleMeaning: to give something (time, money, ideas) to a shared effort

sentences.interview: I contribute meaningfully to every project.
sentences.formal: Let us contribute to the common good.
sentences.informal: Let's all contribute — even a little helps!

words.usageNote: 'Contribute to' = help achieve something. 'Contribution' is the noun.

words.simpleMeaning: to make certain that something will happen

sentences.daily: Let's ensure everything is in order.
sentences.interview: I ensure accuracy in all my work.
sentences.formal: Let us ensure compliance at all times.
sentences.informal: Let's ensure nobody gets left behind.

words.usageNote: 'Ensure' vs 'insure': ensure = make sure, insure = take insurance. Common confusion.

words.simpleMeaning: to deal with a problem or speak to a group

sentences.interview: I address problems proactively.
sentences.business: Let's address the gap in the market.
sentences.formal: Let us address this matter formally.
sentences.informal: Let's address it before it gets worse.

words.usageNote: 'Address a problem/concern/issue' = deal with it formally. Also: 'address the audience'.

words.simpleMeaning: open, clear, and honest in communication

sentences.office: Let's be transparent about the setbacks.
sentences.interview: I work in a transparent and ethical way.
sentences.formal: Let us operate in a transparent manner.
sentences.informal: Let's just be transparent with each other.

words.usageNote: 'Transparency' (noun) is a core business value. 'Maintain transparency' is a common phrase.

words.simpleMeaning: taking action before problems arise

sentences.daily: Let's be proactive about our health.
sentences.office: Let's be proactive and fix it now.
sentences.interview: I am a proactive team member.
sentences.formal: Let us be proactive in addressing risks.
sentences.informal: Let's be proactive — don't wait for problems!

words.usageNote: 'Proactive' is a key word in professional English. Opposite: reactive.

words.simpleMeaning: general agreement among a group

sentences.interview: I know how to build consensus in a team.
sentences.business: Let's find a consensus on pricing.
sentences.formal: Let us arrive at a consensus.
sentences.informal: Let's get a consensus — majority wins!

words.usageNote: 'Reach a consensus', 'build consensus', 'by consensus' are standard phrases.

words.simpleMeaning: a list of items to be discussed at a meeting

sentences.daily: Let's set an agenda for today.
sentences.interview: I always come with a clear agenda.
sentences.business: Let's share the agenda before the meeting.
sentences.formal: Let us adhere to the agenda.
sentences.informal: Let's set an agenda — or we'll never finish!

words.usageNote: 'Agenda item' = single point on the agenda. 'Hidden agenda' = secret motive.

words.simpleMeaning: the result or effect of an action

sentences.daily: Let's focus on the desired outcome.
sentences.office: Let's evaluate the outcome of the campaign.
sentences.interview: I focus on delivering positive outcomes.
sentences.formal: Let us measure outcomes against targets.
sentences.informal: Let's hope for a good outcome!

words.usageNote: 'Outcome' is result-focused. 'Outcome-based' is a key phrase in education and business.

words.simpleMeaning: an important event or stage in a project

sentences.interview: I achieved key milestones in my last role.
sentences.formal: Let us acknowledge this important milestone.
sentences.informal: Let's celebrate the milestone — we earned it!

words.usageNote: 'Project milestone', 'hit a milestone', 'celebrate a milestone' are common uses.

words.simpleMeaning: a particular way of thinking about something

sentences.daily: Let's see it from a new perspective.
sentences.interview: I consider different perspectives.
sentences.business: Let's view this from the customer's perspective.
sentences.formal: Let us consider all perspectives.
sentences.informal: Let's get some perspective here!

words.usageNote: 'From a business/customer perspective' is a very common professional phrase.

words.simpleMeaning: a promise or obligation to do something

sentences.daily: Let's show commitment to our goals.
sentences.office: Let's honor our commitments to clients.
sentences.interview: My commitment to quality is strong.
sentences.formal: Let us reaffirm our commitment.
sentences.informal: Let's show some commitment here, team!

words.usageNote: 'Commitment to' = dedicated to. 'Make a commitment' = promise to do something.

words.simpleMeaning: to reach agreement through discussion

sentences.interview: I am an effective negotiator.
sentences.formal: Let us negotiate a mutually beneficial agreement.
sentences.informal: Let's negotiate — no need to fight over it!

words.usageNote: 'Negotiate a deal/contract/salary' are key business phrases.

words.simpleMeaning: to distribute resources for a specific purpose

sentences.daily: Let's allocate time for this task.
sentences.interview: I know how to allocate tasks fairly.
sentences.formal: Let us allocate responsibilities clearly.
sentences.informal: Let's allocate some time for fun too!

words.usageNote: 'Allocate resources/budget/tasks/time' are common office collocations.

words.simpleMeaning: to make practical use of something

sentences.interview: I know how to utilize data effectively.
sentences.formal: Let us utilize every available opportunity.
sentences.informal: Let's utilize what we have!

words.usageNote: 'Utilize' is more formal than 'use'. In academic/professional writing, 'utilize' is preferred.

words.simpleMeaning: clear and honest in communication

sentences.interview: I believe in transparent communication.
sentences.formal: Let us be fully transparent.
sentences.informal: Let's be transparent — no hidden agendas!

words.usageNote: Repeated because transparency is central to professional English. Key word.

words.simpleMeaning: to change to fit new conditions

sentences.daily: Let's adapt to the new routine.
sentences.office: Let's adapt our approach for this client.
sentences.interview: I adapt quickly to new environments.
sentences.formal: Let us adapt our methods accordingly.
sentences.informal: Let's adapt — the world is changing!

words.usageNote: 'Adapt to' = adjust to a new situation. 'Adaptable' is the adjective.

words.simpleMeaning: the ability to wait calmly

sentences.office: Let's have patience with new employees.
sentences.interview: Patience is one of my key strengths.
sentences.formal: Let us exercise patience and diligence.
sentences.informal: Let's have patience — good things take time!

words.usageNote: 'Have patience', 'lose patience', 'test patience' — common collocations.

words.simpleMeaning: the speed and force with which something moves or progresses

sentences.office: Let's build momentum before the quarter ends.
sentences.interview: I maintain momentum on long projects.
sentences.business: Let's create momentum in the market.
sentences.formal: Let us sustain the current momentum.
sentences.informal: Let's keep the momentum — we're doing great!

words.usageNote: 'Build momentum', 'maintain momentum', 'lose momentum' are key collocations.

words.simpleMeaning: to make something clearer or easier to understand

sentences.interview: I always clarify expectations at the start.
sentences.formal: Let us clarify the terms of the agreement.
sentences.informal: Let's clarify this before we start arguing!

words.usageNote: 'Clarify' is essential in professional communication. 'I'd like to clarify...' is very polite.

words.simpleMeaning: to introduce new ideas or methods

sentences.daily: Let's innovate in our daily habits.
sentences.interview: I constantly look to innovate.
sentences.formal: Let us continuously innovate.
sentences.informal: Let's innovate — the old way isn't working!

words.usageNote: 'Innovate' is key in tech and business. 'Innovation' (noun), 'innovative' (adjective).

words.simpleMeaning: a standard against which things are measured

sentences.daily: Let's set a benchmark for ourselves.
sentences.interview: I benchmark my work against best practices.
sentences.formal: Let us establish clear benchmarks.
sentences.informal: Let's set a benchmark — aim high!

words.usageNote: 'Benchmarking' (process noun) is comparing performance to industry standards.

words.simpleMeaning: to work jointly with others

sentences.daily: Let's collaborate on the art exhibition.
sentences.office: Let's collaborate with the design team.
sentences.interview: I love to collaborate across functions.
sentences.business: Let's collaborate to win more clients.
sentences.formal: Let us collaborate for shared success.
sentences.informal: Let's collaborate — it'll be twice as good!

words.usageNote: 'Collaborate with someone on something' is the standard structure.

words.simpleMeaning: the last date/time for completing a task

sentences.office: Let's not let the deadline slip.
sentences.interview: I never miss a deadline.
sentences.business: Let's push to meet the deadline.
sentences.formal: Let us complete this before the deadline.
sentences.informal: Let's hustle — deadline is tomorrow!

words.usageNote: 'Meet a deadline' (on time) vs 'miss a deadline' (late). Always say 'by the deadline'.

words.simpleMeaning: able to change easily to suit different conditions

sentences.office: Let's be flexible with the approach.
sentences.interview: I am flexible and open to change.
sentences.formal: Let us remain flexible in our approach.
sentences.informal: Let's be flexible — plans can change!

words.usageNote: 'Flexible working', 'flexible approach', 'remain flexible' are common professional phrases.

words.simpleMeaning: able to continue over a long period without damaging resources

sentences.interview: I support sustainable business models.
sentences.formal: Let us commit to sustainable development.
sentences.informal: Let's go sustainable — good for the planet!

words.usageNote: 'Sustainable development/growth/energy' are key 21st-century business terms.

words.simpleMeaning: having a strong desire to achieve great things

sentences.daily: Let's be ambitious with our goals.
sentences.interview: I am ambitious and results-driven.
sentences.formal: Let us set ambitious yet achievable goals.
sentences.informal: Let's be ambitious — why aim low?

words.usageNote: 'Ambitious goal/target/plan' — collocates well with goal-related nouns.

words.simpleMeaning: based on what is actually possible

sentences.daily: Let's be realistic about our goals.
sentences.interview: I set realistic but challenging goals.
sentences.formal: Let us establish realistic timelines.
sentences.informal: Let's be realistic — we can't do it all!

words.usageNote: 'Realistic expectations', 'realistic goals', 'realistic budget' are very common collocations.

words.simpleMeaning: including everyone; not excluding any group

sentences.interview: I promote inclusive practices.
sentences.business: Let's reach a more inclusive customer base.
sentences.formal: Let us build an inclusive organization.
sentences.informal: Let's be inclusive — everyone matters!

words.usageNote: 'Inclusive' is central to DEI (Diversity, Equity, Inclusion) — important in modern workplaces.

words.simpleMeaning: done according to a fixed plan or system

sentences.interview: I work in a systematic and organized manner.
sentences.formal: Let us adopt a systematic methodology.
sentences.informal: Let's be systematic — no more chaos!

words.usageNote: 'Systematic approach' is a key phrase in interviews and professional communication.

words.simpleMeaning: when everyone in a group agrees

sentences.office: Let's aim for a unanimous vote.
sentences.interview: I build unanimous support for key decisions.
sentences.business: Let's get unanimous agreement from the board.
sentences.formal: Let us make this decision unanimous.
sentences.informal: Let's be unanimous on this one!

words.usageNote: 'Unanimous decision/vote/agreement' — used when 100% of people agree.

words.simpleMeaning: acting in advance to deal with problems

sentences.daily: Let's be proactive about our learning.
sentences.interview: I am a proactive and self-driven employee.
sentences.business: Let's take a proactive approach to risk.
sentences.formal: Let us be proactive in our planning.
sentences.informal: Let's be proactive — don't wait for problems to appear!

words.usageNote: 'Proactive' vs 'reactive' — highly valued in interviews. 'Take a proactive approach' is golden phrase.
